text: Highlight Clip Journalist: Flaunting Bitcoin makes you a target for Wrench Attacks On May 22, 2026, bitcoin journalist Joe Nakamoto advised crypto investors in his video to stop talking about Bitcoin and avoid flexing their assets on social media or at dinner parties. He states that this crisis is not unique to France, and publicly discussing crypto assets easily makes one a target for physical wrench attacks. However, he believes the existence of such attacks ironically proves Bitcoin's true value, directly refuting ECB President Christine Lagarde's remark that crypto assets have "no intrinsic value." Finally, he states that crypto users do not need to live in fear and should actively organize defenses, improve security setups, and reduce exposure. — link

text: Zcash founder Zooko Wilcox said security researcher Taylor Hornby discovered a critical counterfeiting vulnerability in Zcash's Orchard pool on May 29. Zcash Open Development Lab coordinated an emergency response, which was completed on June 2. Shielded Labs said the bug was real and exploitable, and a local test exploit could generate unlimited, undetectable counterfeit ZEC. Due to Orchard's privacy design, it cannot be cryptographically proven whether the bug was exploited before being fixed, though Shielded Labs believes prior exploitation was unlikely. It is exploring a network upgrade to verify the integrity of Zcash's supply and prove the non-existence of counterfeit ZEC in the Orchard pool. As of writing, ZEC is trading at $443.05, down 29.0% in the past 24 hours. — link

text: INTEL: Zcash founder confirms a critical Orchard bug that could have created unlimited, undetectable counterfeit ZEC was live from May 2022 until it was patched June 1. Due to the pool's privacy, there is no way to prove whether it was ever exploited. A proposed network upgrade adding a new shielded pool + turnstile accounting, to let anyone verify the integrity of the ZEC supply. $ZEC fell ~25% on the disclosure. The bug that was undetected for ~4 years was found using Anthropic's Opus 4.8, released just days earlier, as part of an AI-assisted security audit. A flaw the world's best cryptographers missed, surfaced by a white-hat using a new AI model. Some argue this is the structural tradeoff of all private money, not a ZEC-specific failure. As X user @ robustus put it, you can't have both full privacy and fully transparent supply auditability in one system. Transparent chains like $BTC expose any inflation bug in the raw data; private chains can't. Zcash's turnstile accounting is designed to limit exactly this. Developing story. Shielded Labs to publish the upgrade proposal next week. More as it comes. Twitter

text: Arrakis: An Honest Assessment of Where Crypto Price Discovery Happens. A cross-venue lead-lag analysis across 29 crypto assets on Hyperliquid, Binance, and Lighter > Finding that Binance led Hyperliquid by ~700ms across all 29 assets studied, while Lighter also led Hyperliquid in 27 of 29 assets, debunking the narrative that Hyperliquid has displaced Binance as the primary price discovery venue. > It argues Hyperliquid's ~700ms lag stems from its fully onchain matching architecture, while faster venues like Binance and Lighter process trades more quickly Source:

text: How to run an active single-LP AMM on EulerSwap without a bot The EulerSwap hook manages an active single-LP position entirely on-chain without an off-chain bot or keeper, executing dynamic asymmetric fees, curvature surcharges, and credit-backed depth automatically through smart contract callbacks Instead of the LP paying to rebalance inventory on external venues, the contract triggers Dutch fee-decay auctions that incentivize arbitrageurs to cover the rebalancing costs when the pool's asset exposure drifts past a configured threshold

text: Bitcoin Drops Over 16% in a Month While S&P 500 Gains 5% Charles Schwab's Jim Ferraioli said Bitcoin's recent underperformance is less about Strategy's small BTC sale and more about BTC losing its momentum-trade appeal. BTC has fallen more than 16% over the past month, while the S&P 500 has gained about 5%, as capital rotates into AI, gold, commodities, hot IPOs and other high-momentum assets. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Highlight Clip Bankless Co-Founder Explains Why He Sold All His ETH Bankless co-founder David Hoffman said in a May 29 interview with Unchained that selling all his ETH was not because he is bearish on Ethereum, nor does he want to be labeled a "defector." He said the decision came down to opportunity cost, as he sees better opportunities elsewhere. Hoffman noted that Bankless was built around the thesis of Ether as money, but he now believes ETH is difficult to re-rate. In his view, Ethereum lacks the strong coordination and execution needed to regain its unique momentum. — link

text: Anthropic looks to set up shop in Singapore, fill roles from finance to product support The successful applicant can expect to receive an annual salary between $307,200 and $331,200 including commissions from sales, said Anthropic on its careers page.

text: FG Nexus Losses on Ethereum Treasury Strategy Top $85 Million Nasdaq-listed Ethereum treasury firm FG Nexus bought 50,770 ETH for about $196 million at an average price of $3,860 between August and September 2025, before beginning to sell in November. It has now sold 36,025 ETH at an average price of $2,330, recovering about $83.92 million, with cumulative losses on its Ethereum treasury strategy exceeding $85 million. FG Nexus previously described ETH as its primary treasury reserve asset. — link

text: Regularization and Bagging: First Techniques in HFT Modelling Standard trading models (OLS) overfit the massive amount of noise in high-frequency trading data, leading to highly unstable predictions and poor performance in live environments. The Solution: Traders combine regularization (which adds a penalty to mathematically constrain model size) and bagging (which averages predictions across multiple randomly sampled versions of the data) to significantly reduce prediction variance and stabilize trading performance

text: Bitmine Immersion Technologies Announces Proposed Series A Perpetual Preferred Stock Offering Bitmine has announced a proposed public offering of 3,000,000 shares of its 9.50% Series A Perpetual Preferred Stock at a stated amount of $100 per share The company intends to use the net proceeds for general corporate purposes, including the acquisition of additional ETH, expanding its staking and validator infrastructure, and repurchasing common stock

text: Polymarket needs to completely overhaul their market resolution system Polymarket has outgrown its decentralized resolution protocol, UMA, which is highly vulnerable to financial manipulation by wealthy token holders because the trading volumes vastly exceed UMA's market cap To fix this, Polymarket needs to internalize its market resolution, hire legal experts to write watertight contracts, introduce deferred settlement windows, and issue refunds for poorly handled markets
